title: RFC-001: Migrate File Service to Otter Storage v2
content: This RFC proposes migrating the file storage backend from the current single-bucket S3 architecture to a multi-tier storage system. Hot files served from S3 Standard, warm files transition to S3-IA after 30 days, cold files archive to Glacier. Estimated 40% cost reduction on storage.

title: RFC-002: CRDT Persistence Strategy for Kelp Sync
content: Proposes a hybrid persistence model for Yjs CRDT documents. Active documents maintain state in Redis with 500ms flush intervals. On document close, full Y.Doc state serialized to PostgreSQL. Handles conflict resolution for offline edits and provides point-in-time document recovery.

title: RFC-003: River Basin Event-Driven Architecture
content: Introduces SNS/SQS event bus for inter-service communication. All state changes published as domain events. Services subscribe to relevant topics. Dead letter queues for failed processing. Event schema registry in shared/events/schemas/. Enables audit trail, search indexing, and analytics without direct service coupling.

title: RFC-004: Dam Builder Rate Limiting Overhaul
content: Proposes replacing the current fixed-window rate limiter in the API gateway with a sliding window approach using Redis sorted sets. Supports per-user, per-endpoint, and per-IP rate limiting. Includes burst allowance for authenticated users and graceful degradation under load.

title: RFC-005: Tidal Pool Caching Layer
content: Adds a distributed caching layer between API gateway and backend services. Uses Redis Cluster with read replicas. Cache invalidation via SNS event subscriptions. TTL-based expiry with cache-aside pattern for document metadata. Expected 60% reduction in database load for read-heavy endpoints.

title: Runbook: Otter Pod Restart Procedure
content: Step-by-step procedure for restarting individual service pods in the OtterWorks EKS cluster. Covers pre-checks (drain connections, health verification), rolling restart commands, post-restart validation, and rollback steps. Includes service-specific notes for stateful services like collab-service and document-service.

title: Runbook: Database Failover (Salmon Run Protocol)
content: Procedure for PostgreSQL primary-replica failover. Covers automated failover via RDS Multi-AZ, manual failover steps, connection string rotation, application reconnection, and data integrity verification. Named Salmon Run because the data swims upstream to the new primary.

title: Q2 2026 Product Roadmap - Operation Kelp Forest
content: Quarterly product roadmap covering AI document summaries, advanced search with filters, bulk file operations, and webhook notifications. Prioritized using RICE scoring. Includes timeline, dependencies, and success metrics. Kelp Forest because we're growing the ecosystem.

title: OtterWorks Design System v3.0 - Ripple UI
content: Comprehensive design system documentation. Covers color palette (ocean blues, kelp greens, sand neutrals), typography scale, spacing system, component library (buttons, cards, modals, tables), accessibility guidelines (WCAG 2.1 AA), and dark mode specifications. Named Ripple because every interaction creates waves.

title: Incident Response Plan - Predator Alert Protocol
content: Incident response procedures for security events. P1-P4 severity classification, escalation paths, communication templates, forensics checklist, and post-incident review process. Named Predator Alert because when threats appear, every otter knows exactly what to do.

title: Welcome to the Holt - New Otter Onboarding Guide
content: Comprehensive onboarding guide for new engineering team members. Covers dev environment setup, codebase walkthrough, service architecture overview, team rituals, communication channels, access provisioning, and first-week checklist. A holt is an otter's home, and this guide helps you find yours.
